title: HCI Hypervisor and AOS Version Compliance
dataSources: Node AOS version, hypervisor version, compliance baseline
spl:
index=hci sourcetype="nutanix:cluster"
| stats latest(aos_version) as aos, latest(hypervisor_version) as hv by node
| lookup hci_compliance_baseline.csv env OUTPUT target_aos, target_hv
| where aos!=target_aos OR hv!=target_hv
| table node, aos, target_aos, hv, target_hv
description: Mixed hypervisor or AOS versions can cause compatibility and support issues. Tracking version compliance supports upgrade planning and support eligibility.
value: Mixed hypervisor or AOS versions can cause compatibility and support issues. Tracking version compliance supports upgrade planning and support eligibility.
implementation: Ingest cluster and node version data. Maintain baseline by environment. Alert on version drift. Report on compliance percentage and nodes due for upgrade.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Nutanix Prism, vSphere/vCenter API.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Node AOS version, hypervisor version, compliance baseline.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Ingest cluster and node version data. Maintain baseline by environment. Alert on version drift. Report on compliance percentage and nodes due for upgrade.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=hci sourcetype="nutanix:cluster"
| stats latest(aos_version) as aos, latest(hypervisor_version) as hv by node
| lookup hci_compliance_baseline.csv env OUTPUT target_aos, target_hv
| where aos!=target_aos OR hv!=target_hv
| table node, aos, target_aos, hv, target_hv
```

Understanding this SPL

**HCI Hypervisor and AOS Version Compliance** — Mixed hypervisor or AOS versions can cause compatibility and support issues. Tracking version compliance supports upgrade planning and support eligibility.

Documented **Data sources**: Node AOS version, hypervisor version, compliance baseline. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Nutanix Prism, vSphere/vCenter API. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: hci; **sourcetype**: nutanix:cluster. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=hci, sourcetype="nutanix:cluster". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by node** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where aos!=target_aos OR hv!=target_hv` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **HCI Hypervisor and AOS Version Compliance**): table node, aos, target_aos, hv, target_hv


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (non-compliant nodes), Pie chart (version distribution), Single value (compliance %).
visualization: Table (non-compliant nodes), Pie chart (version distribution), Single value (compliance %).
